name: Shalom Center For T.R.E.E. Of Life
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing counseling agency offering free homebuyer education, financial counseling, and down payment assistance to low-to-moderate income families in LA and Orange County.
description_long:
Shalom Center for T.R.E.E. of Life is a 501(c)(3) faith-based non-profit organization founded in 1997 that specializes in comprehensive housing counseling and advocacy services. The organization is HUD-approved and focuses specifically on serving low- and moderate-income homebuyers and tenants across Los Angeles County and Orange County. Their mission centers on revitalizing neighborhoods by providing accessible pathways to homeownership and stable housing.

The organization offers a broad range of free and low-cost services including HUD-certified homebuyer education courses, pre-purchase and post-purchase counseling, mortgage delinquency assistance, financial literacy programs (IDEA Program), down payment assistance programs for both LA/OC and San Diego regions, and a food bank service. They provide credit consultation, housing counseling certificates, and specialized resources for fire-affected communities. The agency operates in multiple languages including English and Korean to serve diverse communities.

Shalom Center distinguishes itself through its faith-based approach to housing revitalization, its dual focus on both homeownership preparation and post-purchase support, and its provision of down payment assistance programs—which goes beyond typical credit counseling to address the practical barriers to homeownership. The organization also hosts annual homeownership fairs and maintains an active community engagement calendar. Their HUD-approval specifically qualifies them to provide federally-recognized housing counseling.

As a legitimate non-profit with established infrastructure and community presence, Shalom Center represents a credible free resource for housing-related financial counseling. The main limitation is geographic scope (primarily LA and OC counties) and the reality that while core counseling is free, some specialized programs may have eligibility requirements or income thresholds typical of non-profit services.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency with federal recognition
Completely free homebuyer education and financial counseling services
Offers down payment assistance programs—not just counseling, but direct financial help
Multi-language support including English and Korean
Comprehensive post-purchase and mortgage delinquency counseling (ongoing support, not just initial guidance)
Faith-based non-profit structure with 501(c)(3) status and transparent mission
Active community engagement with annual homebuyer fairs and specialized programs for fire-affected residents
cons: Limited geographic service area (primarily Los Angeles and Orange County; San Diego coverage appears limited)
Website lacks detailed pricing/fee structure and specific eligibility requirements for all programs
No online counseling scheduling or intake system visible; requires phone contact (213-380-3700)
Down payment assistance programs appear to have income/qualification restrictions (typical of non-profits but may exclude some households)
Limited information about counselor credentials, wait times, or response timelines on website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in LA/OC with low-to-moderate income seeking free pre-purchase counseling
Homeowners facing mortgage delinquency or post-purchase financial difficulties
Spanish or Korean-speaking families needing culturally accessible housing guidance
Residents seeking down payment assistance combined with financial education
diagnosis: Shalom Center is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers and struggling homeowners in Los Angeles or Orange County seeking free, non-profit housing counseling with HUD approval. The main caveat is that while core counseling is free, down payment assistance programs have income eligibility requirements, and service is geographically limited to specific California regions.
services: Down payment assistance programs (Los Angeles, Orange County, and San Diego)
LA fire recovery resources and counseling
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2975 Wilshire Blvd, Suite 415, Los Angeles, CA 90010
